content: They rolled it back because player feedback did not appreciate it, so they implemented it on less invasive ways.

At the moment no paid stuff have ethereal pulls. Might have been an oversight. 

If there are such cases in the future, then riot
If the point is to combat bots, what's the point of adding it to paid bundles? At that point don't implement it at all

Because they also said it is their way to give rewards in bigger bulks without abruptly breaking their economy, it should allow future packs to get better/cheaper value, of course said possibility must be seen actually practiced first, the concept of degrading saved pulls to stop account resells and the massive wait times this stagnant accounts  goes through sounds sound on paper, but its only really issue was that it steps on actual players saving on a game that is dupe heavy and is expected to e3 minimum a new fav character, now that it is less invasive and a system that players can actually choose whether to get/receieve or not means there should be little qualms about its existence now.

Mate, no one complained about how this are now, they complained about how they were when they were changing event rewards to temporary too. As Angbak said, we'll see when they add said bundles.

It still doesn't add up, cause those botting and selling accounts don't buy bundles, so they can give better bundles without changing them to temporary anyway. To me it sounds like they want to turn the preorders to temporary pulls because that's what everyone recommends as a must buy, but right now it's speculation until we see the next preorder.

The other guy is shaming other players for complaining when they wanted to change the event rewards to temporary, which, as you said, would have been a major blow considering this game is very dupe reliant. That is not cool.

? All this issues had been rollbacked, of course no one is complaining about the now because it is their answer to the agressive feedback from BEFORE, they listened and had heard it would be very disatisfactory, this way they are still able to use the resources they did by creating Ethereals , it does not matter if it adds up to your head or not because that is what *they* thought was the answer to *their* issue that *they* saw as troublesome for *them* in the long run, now that they are here, it is time to wait and see how they set it up in practice, they *can* change the preorder and other high value packs to ethereals, but only if it did not create a noticable bulk (15+ increase in cores personally) then can the community once again aggressively remind the company of what they said before, and to actually stay true to their promise, or actively decline their playerbase as punishnent for their consistent lying.
Maybe later they add pack with Ethereal . So same price Ethereal pack give more roll than Lunious pack <:obama:582488224086097933>

To me fighting grey market is a huge waste of time and resources, and seems to fall in the same fallacy as piracy that corporations keep falling into and never learns because their shareholders are crying FOMO.

Better yet, if there are people willing to pay for a good starting account, then take inspiration and learn from the grey market and offer a similar one-time bundle and move on, rather than make everyone's life worse.

Last time i checked . With 17$ u will get 1 account :
- 500+ limited roll
- 200 standard roll
- Random SSR
- 2 limited selector 
- Story stop at Chapter 2-1 
Less price just less limited roll ... And i know a some ppl at other group start this game they vhoice buy these acc than top-up to get for same amount
any chance to release the game on nintendo switch 2?

I love focusing on every promise that wasn't delivered. Completely ignoring those that weren't.

Or completely ignoring the Devs possibly changing their mind mid development with more thought.

Yeah that's why until I see stuff ingame. I consider them just on the spur ideas.

Look how long it took limbus to put the facade thing for IDs and it had been showcased like... 8 or so months ago if not more.

But I don't overthink if they add "JP" ultra and the others if they provide interesting things
Which recent promises were delivered?

We can start from, arcane dominion sweeping. Gamemode one time achievements. All rewards not being tied to leaderboard, now we get more from leaderboards, if one decides to bother. The entire skin gacha talk, and the 1 time we had the skin gacha the rewards being reasonable.

They still continue to upkeep the core gameplay well, characters of varied playstyles, even simpler ones. 

These are the more recent/non throwaway answers I have. But if we take everything that light had spoken about early, From VA's to QOL stuff. To even simplifying the unit design akin to the JP version. It also all adds up. 

As i've said here at the start. Taking every word from the director who says and changes his mind repeatedly, is dumb. For a lack of a better word. He keeps doing it unprofessionally... yes. But at the same time, IT happens over and over. The only one who can change is you.

Not adding to the once again. THE LIMITED CORES LASTING 3 MONTHS.

Maybe I'm in a strange position.

Cause I think the current state of the game is underwhelming in comparison to year one. Wich had fully fleshed out events with mechanics, enemies and Multiple CG's. What the story was. The boss design actually playing around mechanics and not only the damage numbers/not being  obnoxious long lead up to a mechanical gotcha moment.

I inevitably, sitting with all characters I want, at dupe levels I want, and still having more pulls, while having spent barely anything playing the game since 2014. I'd do a lot to get the game to the state of quality that made me fall for it in the first place. And inevitably, for the Devs to even have the budget to work on the content they promised

Inevitably playing this game for so long. Seeing how the changes play out. Seeing the Devs listen to not inflating the A5 hp anymore... I legitimately, cannot see and don't believe the Devs have a reason to make an under thought bad change that would "ruin the game" and be worth quitting over. (This is in regards to the AA)

seeing the devs listen to not inflating the A5 hp anymore? rofl are you blind?
or let me guess, 325 score casual talking about A5 content he's never done?
<:24_Clown:1468259444423921871>
<@1487626955791663208> Hey light, may we ask you, Is the A5 Hp slowly increasing numbers based on any player performance metrics or something?

Even though this is usually considered self-evident in other games, I still want to do my best to be transparent and walk you through our thinking.

Over the long term, yes, the numbers do trend upward, and this has been happening continuously. There are a lot of factors at play. For example, in certain versions or phases, HP was substantially lowered due to an unintended technical error, which meant we later had to gradually dial it back up.That re-adjustment happened to fall right before GMurphy’s release, which was awkward timing. The other factor is maintaining challenge: as players gradually strengthen their teams and their combat strategies keep evolving, enemies become relatively weaker in comparison. Of course, we’d want players’ overall power to grow faster than enemies’. Once it reaches a certain threshold, we consider introducing a new difficulty tier or compressing existing difficulties and raising their rewards, which has happened a few times. The original highest score used to be 350.

I can’t promise that numerical inflation won’t happen. But we try to slow it down through differentiated specialties and mechanics, aiming to bring freshness and fun rather than just pure stat increases, and we’ll always maintain older characters so they can reasonably keep up with the evolving environment.

We loved those year-one event mechanics too, and the workload on them came close to main-story territory. A lot of the event mechanics and combat back then, I was building myself. But I’ve had to shift that energy toward marketing, PR, communication, rebuilding the team through hiring, and management. Starting this year, I’ve been getting more involved in character design again, especially with Vortice and Arachne most recently, where I’ve been deeply hands-on, working alongside the combat designers to reclaim the kind of fun the game used to have.

Our investment in combat this past year hasn’t been enough. We tried to free up what budget we could to support story and art, which meant cutting in other places. Once updates get back into a healthy, stable rhythm, gameplay design will gradually catch up too.

How are new players without all that investement supposed to catch up with the train (catch up with the hp inflation and be able to consistently clear the hardest difficulties, or do multiple clears if they desire) if it keeps moving?

I don't think they're supposed to. Not without playing for around a year.

The only way new players get to clear the hardest... We'll "spyral abyss-like" content is purely because it's designed to be made easy via a new unit.

When it comes to an actual investment/gameplay understanding check...

You can't really have best of both worlds

The rewards you lose out on, are some plugs and emote and pfp tickets... It adds up I know... But like... Eh...
